The more pleasure we find in God’s ways, the more we will desire to continue in them. The apostle intended to establish and comfort the Thessalonians regarding the object of their faith—that Jesus Christ was the Savior of the world—and as to the reward of faith, which was more than enough to compensate for all their losses and reward all their work. But he feared his work would be fruitless. If the devil cannot prevent ministers from working in the word and teaching, he will, if possible, hinder the success of their work. No one wants to work without result. It is God’s will and purpose that we enter his kingdom through many trials. And the apostles, instead of encouraging people with the expectation of worldly prosperity in religion, told them plainly they must expect trouble in this life. In this, they followed the example of their great Master, the Author of our faith. Christians were in danger, and they should be warned; they will thus be kept from being deceived by any tricks of the tempter.

Thankfulness to God is very imperfect in our current situation; but one important goal of teaching the word is to help faith grow. That which was used to gain faith is also the means of increasing and confirming it, namely, God’s ordinances; and just as faith comes by hearing, so it is confirmed by hearing as well.

Prayer is religious worship, and all religious worship is due to God alone. Prayer should be offered to God as our Father. Prayer is not only to be offered in the name of Christ, but offered up to Christ himself, as our Lord and Savior. Let us acknowledge God in all that we do, and he will direct our paths. Mutual love is required of all Christians. And love comes from God, and fulfills both the gospel and the law. We need the Spirit’s influence for growth in grace; and the way to obtain it is prayer. Holiness is required of all who want to go to heaven; and we must act so that we do not contradict our profession of holiness. The Lord Jesus will certainly come in his glory; his saints will come with him. Then the excellence as well as the necessity of holiness will be clear; and without this, no hearts will be established at that day, nor will anyone escape condemnation.
